refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs focusing solely on what the system must do without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate an analysis report or data set describing the terrain characteristics of the worksite area independently from other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area
Extract and classify key site features relevant to planning and execution
Integrate safety and operational policy constraints into the terrain analysis
Provide an interface to share the worksite model/map with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model independently, which can then be used by other functions. This supports modularity and reduces coupling between terrain modeling and other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that the terrain model can be generated without tightly coupling to specific planning or execution modules
Risk that integration of safety and operational policies may require additional DPs or tighter coupling if not handled separately
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing the FR further to explicitly separate terrain modeling from feature extraction and policy integration to maintain low coupling and clear module boundaries.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions including terrain modeling for the worksite area.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data from the Vehicle to generate the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed terrain model data to other modules or systems for operational use.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as terrain modeling is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well defined.
Vehicle primarily provides raw data; implementing terrain modeling here would mix sensing and processing, causing diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring, not data modeling; poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services are unrelated to terrain modeling; containment is very weak and diffusion would be high.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation, as it centralizes data processing and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module.
If terrain model data is needed by multiple modules, interface management must ensure minimal coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain' by producing a digital representation of the terrain, which is a clear and verifiable functional decomposition.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within a single IM boundary. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the acceptance criteria that require a worksite model/map to be generated and available to planning/execution functions, which can be tested by checking the output of the NGHE Solution module and its interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly owns the implementation of the terrain model generation, with well-defined interfaces for input (Telemetry) and output (Tasking), ensuring clear allocation of responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts to ensure minimal coupling when terrain model data is consumed by multiple modules.
Monitor and mitigate risks related to dependency on telemetry data timeliness and accuracy from the Vehicle module.
Consider adding monitoring or fallback mechanisms within NGHE Solution to handle telemetry data delays or inaccuracies.

decision.justification: The current functional decomposition is verified as correct and feasible, with the DP directly satisfying the FR and the IM boundary well encapsulating the DP. Verification feasibility and clear ownership are established, and diffusion risks are low and manageable. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level and focus on monitoring and mitigating identified risks rather than further decomposing at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts and add monitoring or fallback mechanisms to handle telemetry data delays or inaccuracies, enhancing robustness and safety.

assumptions: Telemetry data from the Vehicle module will be timely and accurate under normal operation.
The NGHE Solution module can implement monitoring and fallback mechanisms effectively.
open_risks: Dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from the Vehicle module.
Potential coupling issues if interface contracts are not well documented.
evidence_needed: Verification results showing the NGHE Solution module produces accurate terrain models under varying telemetry data conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and monitoring/fallback mechanisms implemented in the NGHE Solution module.
